$comment: Growth-halt budgets for large public root barrels (MC-7 / DZUPAGENT-CODE-L-05 + ARCH-LOW-01)
and per-file LOC ceilings for god-module-prone packages (MC-5 / DZUPAGENT-CODE-L-04).
Each budget is pinned at the CURRENT measured baseline so the gate is green today and
RED on any growth. New public API must land on a documented subpath export
(see config/public-api-allowlists.json subpaths) instead of widening the root barrel.
Metrics:
maxRootBarrelExplicitExports - named re-exports counted across `export { ... } from` blocks in src/index.ts
maxRootBarrelStarExports     - `export * from` statements in src/index.ts
maxRootBarrelSourceCount     - distinct modules re-exported from src/index.ts
maxRootIndexLines            - line count of src/index.ts
auxiliarySourceLineBudgets   - per-file line caps for specific non-index source modules re-exported by the barrel
maxFileLines                 - per-file LOC ceiling applied to every src/**/*.ts (excl. tests/.d.ts) in the package
fileLineAllowlist            - package-relative paths exempt from maxFileLines (pre-existing debt, pinned)
Removing or renaming a root export is a published-consumer breakage and is NOT how you lower these numbers;
lower them only by relocating a cluster to a subpath while keeping a root re-export, which leaves counts flat.
Lower maxFileLines debt by extracting cohesive sub-modules, then shrinking the allowlist.
2026-06-23 Phase-0 re-pin (audit full-dzupagent-2026-06-23/run-001): @dzupagent/core budgets ratcheted
876->885/136->138/1238->1256 for intentional shipped root exports (BaseChatModel re-export 5620e656,
MCP stdio hardening, runtime primitives); relocating them now would break consumers per the rule above.
flow-ast/types.ts aux budget 828->1174 is DEBT-PINNED pending the MC-5 sub-module extraction
(DZUPAGENT-CODE-M-08) — shrink it when that lands. flow-ast validate/document.ts was DEBT-PINNED under the
same track until ARCH-M-06 decomposed it (682->125 LOC) into validate/document/ leaf modules
(durability/policy/inputs/defaults), so its fileLineAllowlist entry was removed.
flow-compiler stages/shape-validate.ts was DEBT-PINNED until the RF-9 refactor (see below) removed it.
2026-06-24 RF-7 (ARCH-H-02): extended barrel governance to every uncovered Tier-1/2 package.
Each newly-added package gets maxRootIndexLines pinned at its current src/index.ts line count and a
maxFileLines:500 per-file ceiling, with current >500-LOC files captured in fileLineAllowlist as pinned
debt (gate green today, RED on any NEW oversized file or root-index growth). Lower each maxRootIndexLines
or shrink each fileLineAllowlist only by extracting cohesive sub-modules — never by removing a published
root export. NOTE: stableRoot/transitionalRoot allowlist entries are Tier-1-only by gate design
(check-package-tiers.mjs FAILS a Tier-2 package that carries root-barrel allowlist rules and REQUIRES every
Tier-1 package to have them); all Tier-1 packages are already governed, so RF-7 adds no new allowlist
entries — barrel-budgets is the correct governance surface for the remaining Tier-2 packages.
2026-06-24 RF-9 (CODE-M-08 / ARCH-M-06): flow-compiler stages/shape-validate.ts refactored to a
data-driven per-node-kind validator table; when the file drops below 500 LOC its fileLineAllowlist entry
is removed (gate then enforces the 500-LOC ceiling on it directly).

ServerRouteMountable + ServerDeclaredServices + MountedServerConfig root exports added by the
narrowed route-plugin seam.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 / MJ-01 assessment (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/agent-adapters src/index.ts (839 LOC, in fileLineAllowlist + maxRootIndexLines:839) was reviewed as the next debt-pinned oversized file after the 4 largest god-modules were decomposed. VERDICT: NO SPLIT. The file is a PURE re-export barrel -- every line 14-839 is an export / export type / export * statement re-exporting from ~80 leaf modules, with ZERO embedded implementation logic (no functions, classes, or business logic). It does not fuse construction/dispatch/runtime concerns (the MJ-01 god-module criterion); it only widens the public surface. Per the ratchet policy above a root export cannot be removed without breaking published consumers, and relocating clusters to sub-barrels leaves maxRootIndexLines flat while adding indirection and re-export-alias risk (e.g. `StuckDetectorConfig as AdapterStuckDetectorConfig`). This mirrors the memory/src/index.ts precedent (also a pure re-export barrel, debt-pinned not split, same date). The 839 baseline was itself deliberately reviewed and pinned in the 2026-07-22 ARCH-M-07 entry above. Leaving the allowlist entry and maxRootIndexLines pin in place is the honest outcome: a wide barrel is governed-and-frozen public surface, not a god-module. reviewBy: 2026-10-22.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 / MJ-01 decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/agent src/pipeline/loop-executor.ts (717 LOC, debt-pinned 2026-06-24 RF-6) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED. VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. The file fused four distinct concerns per the MJ-01 criterion (construction/dispatch/runtime logic mixed together): the predicate-based iteration algorithm (executeLoop), the bounded-concurrency for_each algorithm with ordered-prefix flushing (executeForEachLoop + 7 aggregation helpers), JSON-path state read/write utilities (resolveStatePath/setStatePath), and the built-in continue-predicate factories (stateFieldTruthy/qualityBelow/hasErrors). Decomposed into src/pipeline/loop-executor/ leaf modules (types.ts=LoopResumeOptions, state-path.ts, for-each-loop.ts, predicate-loop.ts, predicates.ts), mirroring the sibling pipeline-runtime-lifecycle/ and tool-handlers/ conventions. The original loop-executor.ts is now a thin barrel (~25 LOC) preserving the EXACT public surface (executeLoop, LoopResumeOptions, stateFieldTruthy, qualityBelow, hasErrors) with zero signature or behavior changes; all internal consumers (pipeline-runtime/loop-node-handler.ts, stage-dispatch.ts) and the src/index.ts / src/pipeline.ts / src/pipeline/index.ts re-export chains resolve unchanged. Every extracted leaf is under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 / MJ-01 decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/flow-compiler src/stages/semantic-walk.ts (677 LOC, debt-pinned in the 2026-07-12 QF-06 batch) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED. VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. Unlike the pure-barrel NO-SPLIT verdicts above, this file fused TWO independent Stage-3 semantic passes into one module (the MJ-01 god-module criterion): (1) the recursive AST traversal `visit` that dispatches each FlowNode variant to its resolver/validator sub-pass, plus a self-contained for_each scalar-export check (validateForEachScalarExports + collectScalarOutputPaths); and (2) a wholly separate two-pass checkpoint/restore cross-node validator `validateCheckpointRestore` with its own recursion and label/id collection (collectCheckpointLabels + walkCheckpointRestore). Decomposed into src/stages/semantic-walk/ leaf modules (dispatch.ts=411 LOC, checkpoint-restore.ts=267 LOC), mirroring the sibling compile-orchestrator/ convention. The original semantic-walk.ts is now a 12-LOC thin re-export composition root preserving the EXACT public surface (visit, validateCheckpointRestore) consumed by stages/semantic.ts, with zero signature or behavior changes. Both extracted leaves are under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 / MJ-01 decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/flow-compiler src/route-target.ts (574 LOC, debt-pinned in fileLineAllowlist) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED. VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. Unlike the pure-barrel NO-SPLIT verdicts above, this file fused THREE independent STAGE-4 AST passes into one module (the MJ-01 god-module criterion of multiple unrelated passes co-located, not one cohesive routing pass): (1) the D2 feature bitmask + router→lowerer dispatch decision (FEATURE_BITS, computeFeatureBitmask, routeTarget) — one recursive OR-ing walk plus the routing rule; (2) a wholly separate forward-compatible on_error structural detection pass (hasOnError) with its own independent recursion, the STAGE-2/STAGE-4 skill-chain backstop; and (3) the runtime-leaf/artifact-anchor pass (collectUnsupportedRuntimeNodes + UnsupportedRuntimeNode, backed by the private RUNTIME_LEAF_NODE_TYPES set, isUnsupportedForTarget, and a separate hasGenericArtifactAnchor recursion) that flags runtime-executed leaves a given target cannot lower. Decomposed into src/route-target/ leaf modules (mirroring the sibling src/stages/semantic-walk/ and src/compile-orchestrator/ per-concern conventions landed the same day): target-router.ts (~207 LOC: FEATURE_BITS, FeatureBitmask, computeFeatureBitmask, routeTarget), on-error.ts (~127 LOC: hasOnError), unsupported-runtime-nodes.ts (~255 LOC: UnsupportedRuntimeNode, collectUnsupportedRuntimeNodes + the two private helpers + RUNTIME_LEAF_NODE_TYPES). The original route-target.ts is now a ~24-LOC thin re-export composition root preserving the EXACT public surface (FEATURE_BITS, routeTarget, computeFeatureBitmask, FeatureBitmask, hasOnError, collectUnsupportedRuntimeNodes, UnsupportedRuntimeNode) consumed via the ./route-target.js path by src/index.ts, src/compile-orchestrator/pipeline.ts, src/stages/shape-validate.ts, src/capability-manifest/requirements.ts, and the __tests__/emit + route-target + compile + compile-runtime-nodes suites, with zero signature or behavior changes (the private helpers were never part of the exported surface). Every extracted leaf is under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 god-module decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/server src/routes/evals.ts (687 LOC) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED. VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. The file fused four concerns inline per the MJ-01 criterion -- pure request validators/filter+meta builders, HTTP error mapping, the ReadOnlyEvalOrchestrator fallback class, and the createEvalRoutes handler wiring. Decomposed (mirrors the ./learning.ts thin-coordinator precedent in the same routes/ dir) into src/routes/evals-helpers.ts (354 LOC: validators, buildRunListFilter/Meta, mapEvalRouteError, ReadOnlyEvalOrchestrator, constants/types) and src/routes/evals-handlers.ts (305 LOC: registerEvalHandlers registers the 7 routes onto a Hono instance). evals.ts is now a 128-LOC composition root that resolves the orchestrator/mode and re-exports the six pure validators (validateRunListQuery, buildRunListFilter, buildRunListMeta, validateCreateRunMetadata, canCancelRun, canRetryRun) plus EvalRouteConfig/EvalOrchestratorFactory types, preserving the exact ./routes/evals import surface consumed by extensions.ts, features.ts, optional-routes.ts, and eval-route-helpers.test.ts. Route paths, request/response contracts, status codes, and SEC-M-06 tenant scoping are unchanged (behavior-preserving). Every file is now under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 god-module decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/flow-ast src/parse/spdd.ts (634 LOC, debt-pinned in fileLineAllowlist) SPLIT and its src/parse/spdd.ts fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED (the sibling src/validate/spdd.ts entry is retained; it is decomposed separately). VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. The file fused thirteen independent per-node SPDD parsers (parseSpddImportSources/BuildSourcePack/RunAnalysis/GenerateCanvas/ValidateCanvas/ReviewCanvas/ProjectPlan/ArmDispatch/RunValidation/CollectProof/ScanDrift/CreateSyncProposal/AgentSwarm) plus three shared private field-validation helpers (requireStringField, requireArrayField, requireSubTasksField) inline in one module -- the MJ-01 criterion of multiple unrelated parsers co-located. Decomposed into src/parse/spdd/ leaf modules grouped by SPDD workflow phase (mirroring the sibling src/validate/document/ per-concern convention): field-helpers.ts (the 3 shared validators, now exported for intra-directory use), sources.ts (import_sources/build_source_pack/run_analysis), canvas.ts (generate/validate/review_canvas + project_plan), dispatch.ts (arm_dispatch/run_validation/collect_proof), and drift-swarm.ts (scan_drift/create_sync_proposal/agent_swarm). The original spdd.ts is now a ~24-LOC thin re-export composition root preserving the EXACT public surface -- all 13 parseSpdd* functions -- consumed by ./dispatch.ts and __tests__/spdd-agent-swarm.spec.ts, with zero signature or behavior changes (the field helpers were never part of the exported surface). Every extracted leaf is under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

Distinct from a pure re-export barrel, the file fused twelve independent per-node-kind SPDD schema validators (validateSpddImportSources/BuildSourcePack/RunAnalysis/GenerateCanvas/ValidateCanvas/ReviewCanvas/ProjectPlan/ArmDispatch/RunValidation/CollectProof/ScanDrift/CreateSyncProposal/AgentSwarm) plus three shared private field-check helpers (requireString, requireArray, requireSubTasks) inline in one module -- the MJ-01 criterion of multiple unrelated validators co-located, not one cohesive pass. 2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 god-module decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/flow-dsl src/normalize-nodes-agent.ts (673 LOC, debt-pinned in fileLineAllowlist) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED (the sibling src/normalize-nodes-spawn-emit-memory.ts entry is retained; it is decomposed separately). VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. The file fused two independent node normalizers plus the entire agent per-field normalizer set inline per the MJ-01 criterion (multiple unrelated node-normalization rules co-located, not one cohesive pass): the `agent` node normalizer (normalizeAgent + AGENT_KEYS) with its six per-field sub-normalizers (normalizeOutput/normalizeStop/normalizeOnInvalidOutput/normalizeRetry+readAttemptsBranch, normalizeValidation, normalizePolicy) and the separate `validate` node normalizer (normalizeValidate + VALIDATE_KEYS), the two sharing only the normalizeCommands helper. Decomposed into src/normalize-nodes-agent/ leaf modules (mirroring the sibling src/format-nodes/ per-concern convention landed the same day): normalize-agent-node.ts (the agent normalizer + AGENT_KEYS), normalize-validate-node.ts (the validate normalizer + VALIDATE_KEYS), agent-output-retry-fields.ts (output/stop/onInvalidOutput/retry field normalizers + the private readAttemptsBranch), and agent-validation-policy-fields.ts (validation/policy field normalizers + the shared normalizeCommands, now exported for intra-directory use). The original normalize-nodes-agent.ts is now a ~19-LOC thin re-export composition root preserving the EXACT public surface (normalizeAgent, normalizeValidate) consumed by ./normalize-node-helpers.ts's node dispatcher, with zero signature or behavior changes (the per-field normalizers and readAttemptsBranch were never part of the exported surface). Every extracted leaf is under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 god-module decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/connectors-browser src/browser/auth-handler.ts (670 LOC, debt-pinned 2026-07-12 QF-06) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED. VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. The AuthHandler class fused three distinct auth strategies plus low-level browser-driver plumbing in one module per the MJ-01 criterion: credential-form login (fillAndSubmitLogin), cookie-seeding (loginWithCookies), and an SSO-pivot/entry-discovery strategy (discoverLoginEntry + enterSsoProvider), layered over post-credential interstitial resolution (resolveAccountPickerInterstitial/resolveInterstitials/readVisibleAlert) and low-level SPA-hydration/navigation-readiness plumbing (waitForSpaReady/waitForLoginNavigationReady/waitForLoginComplete) plus the full-flow orchestrator (submitAndVerify/performLogin) -- not one cohesive flow. The class holds no instance state, so each private helper was extracted to a free-standing pure function. Decomposed into src/browser/auth-handler/ leaf modules (mirroring the sibling agent/loop-executor/ and flow-compiler/semantic-walk/ per-concern conventions): selectors.ts (selector catalogues + timeout bounds), readiness.ts (SPA/navigation-ready/login-complete waits), credential-form.ts (username/password fill+submit), cookies.ts (cookie-seeding), discovery.ts (isLoginPage/discoverLoginEntry/enterSsoProvider), interstitials.ts (account-picker/consent resolution + alert read), perform-login.ts (submit->verify->SSO-pivot orchestration). The original auth-handler.ts is now a 94-LOC thin class-façade composition root preserving the EXACT public surface (AuthHandler class with loginWithCredentials/loginWithCookies/isLoginPage/discoverLoginEntry/performLogin) consumed by src/browser-connector.ts, the src/index.ts root re-export, __tests__/auth-handler.test.ts, and the external testman-app AuthHandler vi.fn mocks, with zero signature or behavior changes (all extracted helpers were private, never part of the exported surface). Every extracted leaf is under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 god-module decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/subagents src/runtime/background-subagent-runtime.ts (655 LOC, debt-pinned in the 2026-07-08 MJ-01 batch) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED (the sibling src/store/postgres-task-store.ts entry is retained; its decomposition is deferred separately). VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split -- but NOT along the class boundary. Unlike a pure re-export barrel, this file fused three separable concerns onto the single BackgroundSubagentRuntime class per the MJ-01 criterion: (1) a ~100-LOC public contract/type surface (GovernanceEventSink + noopGovernanceSink, SpawnOutcome, SpawnBatchAdmission, SubagentAdmission{Resolution,Resolver}, SpawnOptions, TaskScope, BackgroundSubagentRuntimeDeps); (2) the admit->run->drain execution/concurrency-accounting half (tryAdmit/drainQueue/resolveApprovalThenAdmit) which owns the per-task AbortController registry and the runner fire-and-forget dispatch; and (3) a standalone crash-recovery routine (reconcileOrphans) that already delegated to the store's recoverStaleRunningTasks. The public spawn/check/await/cancel/list/evaluateBatch API loop is one cohesive coordinator and was deliberately KEPT WHOLE (splitting its methods would leak shared private state) -- mirroring how the sibling store/postgres-task-store.ts keeps persistence separate, the honest seam here is the contract surface + the two detachable sub-routines, not the coordinator loop. Decomposed into src/runtime/background-subagent-runtime/ leaf modules (mirroring the sibling lifecycle-controller.ts convention): contracts.ts (122 LOC: all public types + noopGovernanceSink), admission-pipeline.ts (161 LOC: AdmissionPipeline class owning the AbortController map + admit/drain/approval methods, injected the same store/runner/gate/events/clock/logger/lifecycle seams via a deps bag), orphan-reconciler.ts (79 LOC: reconcileOrphans free function over an OrphanReconcilerContext seam bag), admission-audit.ts (18 LOC: defaultAuditForSpec), sleep.ts (10 LOC: unref'd poll timer). The LifecycleController abort callback now resolves this.pipeline.abort(taskId) lazily (the pipeline owns the controller registry). The original background-subagent-runtime.ts is now a 453-LOC composition root that constructs the pipeline, delegates spawn's admit/approval branches + cancel's abort to it, delegates reconcileOrphans to the leaf, and RE-EXPORTS the exact public type surface so the ./runtime/background-subagent-runtime.js import path is unchanged for every consumer (index.ts, create-runtime.ts, orchestrator-background-api.ts, tools/subagent-tools.ts, tools/fanout/{types,item-pipeline}.ts, and the __tests__ helpers/integration/logger-seam suites). Zero signature or behavior changes. Every file is now under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

2026-07-22 ARCH-M-06 god-module decomposition (audit full-dzupagent-2026-07-21/run-001): @dzupagent/agent src/agent/run-engine.ts (600 LOC, debt-pinned in fileLineAllowlist) SPLIT and its fileLineAllowlist entry REMOVED. VERDICT: GOD-MODULE, split. Distinct from a pure re-export barrel (though the file had already off-loaded its heaviest phase bodies to sibling run-engine-*.ts helpers), the module still fused four unrelated top-level run-engine concerns inline per the MJ-01 criterion of multiple independent orchestrators co-located: (1) run-state PREPARATION -- applySamplingOptions + prepareRunState (guardrail/budget resolution, message prepare + journal rehydration, OWASP prompt-injection/PII scan, beforeModelCall hook dispatch, prompt-cache marker injection, tool-issuance permission gate, model bind) plus its two private helpers scanHumanMessages/extractFirstHumanMessage; (2) the GENERATE-run orchestrator executeGenerateRun with its ApprovalSuspendedError->suspended-result branch and the failed-run snapshot path (+ private executeGenerateRunInner); (3) RESULT/TELEMETRY utilities applyOutputFilter/emitStopReasonTelemetry/createToolStatTracker; and (4) the STREAMING tool-call orchestrator executeStreamingToolCall (5-phase gate/invoke/success/error stack) -- four seams that share no state, only the run-engine namespace. Decomposed into src/agent/run-engine/ leaf modules (mirroring the pre-existing sibling src/agent/run-engine/types.ts and the agent/loop-executor/, agent/workflow/compiled-workflow/ per-concern conventions): prepare-state.ts (359 LOC: applySamplingOptions + prepareRunState + the 2 private scan/extract helpers), generate-run.ts (95 LOC: executeGenerateRun + private executeGenerateRunInner), result-telemetry.ts (61 LOC: applyOutputFilter/emitStopReasonTelemetry/createToolStatTracker), streaming-tool-call.ts (98 LOC: executeStreamingToolCall). The original run-engine.ts is now a 44-LOC thin re-export composition root preserving the EXACT public surface -- applySamplingOptions, prepareRunState, executeGenerateRun, applyOutputFilter, emitStopReasonTelemetry, createToolStatTracker, executeStreamingToolCall, the re-exported DEFAULT_UNGUARDED_BUDGET/DEFAULT_GUARDED_MAX_ITERATIONS constants, the StreamingToolExecutionResult/StreamingToolPolicyOptions/ToolStatTracker type re-exports, and `export type * from './run-engine/types.js'` -- consumed by the sibling streaming-run*.ts drivers (streaming-run.ts, streaming-run-iteration.ts, streaming-run-policy.ts, streaming-run-tool-handler.ts, streaming-run-fallback.ts) and the __tests__ suites (run-engine, run-engine-resume, scan-human-messages, track-phase-wiring, tool-result-prompt-injection, compression-log, output-filter, run-engine-streaming-helpers), with zero signature or behavior changes (scanHumanMessages/extractFirstHumanMessage/executeGenerateRunInner were always private, never part of the exported surface). Every extracted leaf is under the 500-LOC maxFileLines ceiling.

Distinct from a pure re-export barrel, the file fused four concerns inline per the MJ-01 criterion: (1) response redaction (redactMcpDefinition + PublicMcpServerDefinition + SENSITIVE_HEADER_PATTERN); (2) payload-size validation (getOversizedMcpServerField + the 64KB/128KB size constants); (3) transport/argument policy checks (validateHttpServerInput URL-policy short-circuit + the forbiddenCommandResponse ForgeError->403 envelope); and (4) the createMcpRoutes handler wiring for 12 routes across the /servers and /profiles resources. Route paths, request/response contracts, status codes, the SEC-H-02 tenant scoping, the SEC-H-02/RF-S03/MJ-SEC-03 stdio allowlist + inline-eval arg policy, the QF-SEC-06 response redaction, and the MCP URL policy are all unchanged (behavior-preserving; the redaction/size/policy helpers were always private, never part of the exported surface). routeEndpointManifest ownership of the 12 /api/mcp endpoints moves from mcp.ts to mcp-handlers.ts (classified compatibility-maintenance), mirroring how evals-handlers.ts owns the manifest and evals-helpers.ts is internal-support.
